title: Bereaved Parent Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Bereaved Parent Support Groups 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Families/Friends of People with Cancer Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CANDLELIGHTERS FOR CHILDREN WITH CANCER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides practical and emotional support services to children and their families who have been affected by childhood cancer. Families are served from diagnosis through treatment, and bereavement through monthly support groups, family activities and events, teen groups, emergency funds, scholarships, and food pantries and meal programs at local hospitals. 10070 SW Beaverton-Hillsdale Highway Suite 360 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Children ages 0-21 who currently have or have had cancer and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 719-4244 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMPASSIONATE FRIENDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers emotional support to families grieving the death of a child of any age from any cause. Provides online and in-person support groups and offers information on grief education and other resources. Local agency chapters may be found using the online locator tool. Eligibility: Parents, grandparents, adult siblings, and anyone else in a guardianship role who are grieving the death of a child Hours: Varies by location Email (877) 969-0010 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES DOUGY CENTER EAST PEER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-ended peer support groups for those grieving a death, as well as groups for individuals living with advanced serious illness. Services are available in-person and online. Also offers specialized information and referral on these topics. 3909 SE 52nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Children and teens ages 3-18 and young adults ages 19-35 and their families who have experienced the death of a parent, primary caregiver, or sibling Hours: Support group times vary Email (503) 775-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES DOUGY CENTER SOUTH PEER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-ended peer support groups for those grieving a death, as well as groups for individuals living with advanced serious illness. Services are available in-person and online. Also offers specialized information and referral on these topics. 350 SE 13th Avenue Canby, OR 97013 Eligibility: Children and teens ages 3-18 and young adults ages 19-35 and their families who have experienced the death of a parent, primary caregiver, or sibling Hours: Support group times vary Email (503) 775-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES DOUGY CENTER WEST PEER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-ended peer support groups for those grieving a death, as well as groups for individuals living with advanced serious illness. Services are available in-person and online. Also offers specialized information and referral on these topics. 13565 SW Walker Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Children and teens ages 3-18 and young adults ages 19-35 and their families who have experienced the death of a parent, primary caregiver, or sibling Hours: Support group times vary Email (503) 775-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST CANDLE BEREAVEMENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers bereavement support for families grieving the loss of a child or infant through one-on-one support, access to informational materials, and referrals to local support services. 21 Locust Avenue Suite 2B New Canaan, CT 06840 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 221-7437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE OPEN COMMUNITY CIRCLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides grief support in a group setting. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 1st and 3rd Thursdays of the month 6pm-7:30pm Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE GRIEF TRAVELERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an ongoing 4 week series for 6-10 participants with weekly 60 minute group sessions. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE INDIVIDUAL GRIEF COUNSELING SESSIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual counseling sessions. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE GRIEF JOURNEY EXTENDED PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers extended grief and bereavement counseling programs two times a year in the spring and fall. Sessions run for 12 weeks up to 12 months depending on the needs of the group. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE TEEN GROUP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe place for teenagers to come and deal with various issues related to grief. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Ages 11-17. Participants must commit to four weeks of the program. Some age exceptions are permitted depending on the maturity of the child. Hours: Wednesday 3pm-4pm Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, support groups, and wellness services to people affected by or at risk for mental health conditions related to parenthood. Support people, including partners and babies under age 1, are welcome at all meetings. 576 Olive Street Suite 205 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families struggling with emotions related to reproductive health, such as pregnancy, the first few years postpartum, and perinatal loss Hours: Support group times vary Email (541) 525-0495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CANDLELIGHTERS FOR CHILDREN WITH CANCER CANDLELIGHTERS FOR CHILDREN WITH CANCER DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES PEER SUPPORT GROUPS DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES PEER SUPPORT GROUPS DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES PEER SUPPORT GROUPS LIVING UNDONE OPEN COMMUNITY CIRCLES WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES
